reason: Changing /etc/gshadow permissions to the STIG‑required mode is a standard file‑permission hardening step. Root bypasses permission checks, so the change will not lock out administrators or break critical services, and it follows the policy for safe remediation of file permissions.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential mis‑application of chmod could leave the file inaccessible to tools that expect group shadow data; Incorrect syntax could revert permissions to an insecure state
